Concept Focus: Pi is architected around a strict engine-interface boundary so reasoning state and user interaction evolve independently.
Connecting Dots: This split explains why context, sessions, and tools remain core concerns while interaction behaviors are handled by the TUI layer.

Concept Focus: The loop acts as a model-guided control cycle where tool selection and turn completion emerge from iterative responses.
Same Idea, Different Layer: Instead of deterministic branching logic, the system relies on repeated context updates plus tool feedback to drive next actions.

Concept Focus: Context initialization is a layered assembly pipeline where ordering directly affects instruction priority and retrieval efficiency.
Connecting Dots: Using compact skill descriptors at setup time anticipates lazy-loading later, preserving token budget without reducing discoverability.

Concept Focus: Compaction is governed by runtime usage signals rather than static token heuristics.
Implications: Long-running sessions remain tractable because summary checkpoints preserve continuity while reducing context payload.
Edge Case: When provider usage telemetry is incomplete, conservative compaction behavior may trigger earlier to avoid overflow risk.

Concept Focus: Tool execution transforms a single prompt into an iterative reasoning-action loop until the model decides no further calls are needed.
Implications: Execution quality depends on reinjecting each tool result into context so subsequent reasoning reflects actual system state.

Concept Focus: JSONL persistence optimizes append throughput and observability by storing each message as an independent line object.
Connecting Dots: This structure pairs naturally with branch lineage metadata, enabling replay and debugging without heavyweight storage systems.

Concept Focus: Parent-linked identifiers encode conversation branching as a graph over a flat event log.
Same Idea, Applied Elsewhere: The pattern mirrors commit DAG design, where immutable history supports safe exploration and reversible decision paths.

Concept Focus: A minimal default toolset favors composability and predictable safety boundaries over broad built-in feature surface.
Implications: Extension-based expansion keeps the trusted core small while allowing specialized capabilities per workflow context.

Concept Focus: Extensions are event-driven adapters that hook lifecycle points without mutating core orchestration semantics.
Connecting Dots: Explicit phase events allow cross-cutting behaviors like custom tools and renderers to be attached modularly.

Concept Focus: The system prompt is intentionally concise, serving as a coordination scaffold rather than an exhaustive rule corpus.
Implications: Prompt minimalism reduces instruction interference and makes targeted augmentation via append files more controllable.

Concept Focus: Startup flow separates command ingestion from orchestration so initialization remains centralized and test-friendly.
Connecting Dots: Session creation occurring after config and extension loading guarantees runtime behavior reflects finalized startup state.

Concept Focus: The TUI uses component-local rendering tied to event updates to prevent global redraw jitter.
Implications: Per-region refresh improves perceived responsiveness during streaming output and concurrent status updates.

Concept Focus: Skills are lazily loaded so rich instruction files remain available on demand without permanently inflating context.
Edge Case: If a referenced skill path is inaccessible at invocation time, execution may degrade to descriptor-only guidance.
